title: Makefile: validate that each commit can at least build
body:
it is very useful when using git bisect that at least the commit can
build.

got inspiration from: https://twitter.com/pid_eins/status/1072797993760423941

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
/approve
LGTM, thanks for the great addition @giuseppe !

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
the new tests are finally passing.  It is failing somewhere else now

> the new tests are finally passing. It is failing somewhere else now

Yay!  Dang, That error doesn't seem related to any changes you made here, is that happening on master or other PRs?
rebased, let's see if it solves the CI failures

@giuseppe running this post-merge on master broke:  https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5157597072064512

Perhaps it can be skipped in that context?  If so, just need to add:

```yaml
build_each_commit_task:
    ...
    only_if: $CIRRUS_BRANCH != 'master'
    ...
```
